title: Private Placement Distribution Compliance
content:
MEMORANDUM TO: Senior Advisors and Private Wealth Team
FROM: Compliance Department
RE: Updated Procedures for Private Placement Distributions

Effective immediately, all senior advisors must adhere to enhanced compliance protocols when presenting private placement opportunities to qualified clients. This memo addresses critical updates to our distribution practices in light of recent SEC guidance and FINRA enforcement actions.

REGULATORY FRAMEWORK

Under SEC Regulation D, private placements offered through Rules 506(b) and 506(c) require strict adherence to accredited investor verification procedures. FINRA Rule 5122 additionally mandates that member firms conduct reasonable due diligence on private placement sponsors and underlying investments before distribution to retail clients. Our firm's enhanced procedures exceed these baseline requirements.

ACCREDITED INVESTOR VERIFICATION

All clients considering private placements must meet accredited investor standards: individual net worth exceeding $1 million (excluding primary residence) or annual income of $200,000 ($300,000 joint) for the past two years with reasonable expectation of continuation. For entity clients, net assets must exceed $5 million or all equity owners must individually qualify as accredited.

Verification documentation must be obtained within 90 days of investment and include: recent tax returns, third-party verification letters from CPAs or attorneys, bank statements, or brokerage account statements. The compliance department has revised Form MW-AI-2025 to streamline this process.

DUE DILIGENCE REQUIREMENTS

Before presenting any private placement, advisors must complete enhanced due diligence using our updated DDQ-PP framework. This includes:

- Sponsor track record analysis covering minimum 7-year performance history
- Independent verification of audited financial statements
- Background checks on all key personnel through regulatory databases
- Assessment of fee structures, ensuring total costs remain below 3.5% annually
- Liquidity analysis confirming alignment with client investment horizons

CONCENTRATION LIMITS

Client alternative investment concentrations may not exceed 20% of total investable assets without Chief Investment Officer approval. For clients with net worth below $2.5 million, this limit decreases to 15%. Single private placement positions are capped at 5% of client portfolios, with exceptions requiring Principal approval and documented justification.

DISCLOSURE OBLIGATIONS

All private placement presentations must include standardized risk disclosures using Form MW-PP-DISC-2025. Key points include illiquidity risks, potential total loss of investment, limited regulatory oversight, and fee transparency. Clients must acknowledge receipt and understanding through documented signatures on disclosure forms.

Advance notice of 48 hours must be provided to the compliance department before scheduling private placement presentations. This allows adequate review time for offering documents and ensures proper client qualification.

RECORDKEEPING

Complete files must be maintained for all private placement solicitations, including declined opportunities. Required documentation includes: client accreditation verification, due diligence summaries, presentation materials, disclosure acknowledgments, and investment committee approvals where applicable.

COMPLIANCE MONITORING

Monthly reporting to the compliance committee will include private placement activity summaries, concentration analyses, and exception reports. Quarterly reviews will assess sponsor performance, client satisfaction metrics, and regulatory developments affecting our distribution practices.

VIOLATION CONSEQUENCES

Failure to comply with these procedures may result in suspension of private placement distribution privileges, regulatory examination exposure, and potential disciplinary action. Recent industry settlements averaging $2.8 million demonstrate regulatory focus on this area.

Questions regarding these procedures should be directed to the compliance department. Additional training sessions will be scheduled for April 2025 covering practical implementation scenarios.

This memo supersedes all previous private placement guidance and remains effective until further notice.
